name: Absorbing Tattoo
entriesTemplate: Produced by a special needle, this magic tattoo features designs that emphasize one color ({{item.detail1}}).

entriesTemplate.name: Tattoo Attunement
entries: To attune to this item, you hold the needle to your skin where you want the tattoo to appear, pressing the needle there throughout the attunement process. When the attunement is complete, the needle turns into the ink that becomes the tattoo, which appears on the skin.
If your attunement to the tattoo ends, the tattoo vanishes, and the needle reappears in your space.

entriesTemplate.name: Damage Resistance
entries: While the tattoo is on your skin, you have resistance to {{item.resist}} damage.

entriesTemplate.name: Damage Absorption
entries: When you take {{item.resist}} damage, you can use your reaction to gain immunity against that instance of the damage, and you regain a number of hit points equal to half the damage you would have taken. Once this reaction is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

name: Armor of Resistance
entriesTemplate: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to {{getFullImmRes item.resist}} damage while you wear this armor.

name: Arrow
entries: Arrows are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Arrows are typically stored in a {@item Quiver|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Arrows (20)
entries: Arrows are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Arrows are typically stored in a {@item Quiver|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Artisan's Tools
entries: Artisan's Tools are each focused on crafting items and pursuing a trade. Each type of Artisan's Tools tools requires a separate proficiency.

If you have proficiency with a tool, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check too.

name: Assault Rifle
entries: Combining a high rate of fire with rifle-grade ballistics, the Assault Rifle is a staple weapon for all modern militaries. Dozens of variants exist on this general design, but all share the traits of a flexible and formidable weapon.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Birdpipes
entries: Pan pipes or satyr pipes, also known as the shalm, these are sacred to {@deity Lliira|Faerûnian|scag} and popular with wood elf and wild elf bards.

name: Blunderbuss
entries: This distinctive short-range firearm features a dramatically flared muzzle designed to launch shot in a wide spray. Most effective at close range, the Blunderbuss can be considered a precursor to the modern shotgun.

name: Bolt
entries: Crossbow bolts are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Bolts are typically stored in a {@item Crossbow Bolt Case|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Bolts (20)
entries: Crossbow bolts are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Bolts are typically stored in a {@item Crossbow Bolt Case|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Bullet
entries: Ammunition is required by a weapon that has {@itemProperty AF|XDMG|Ammunition}. A weapon's description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon.
Firearm ammunition is destroyed upon use.

name: Cannon
entries: Smoothbore, muzzleloading Cannons are common fixtures on pirate ships and defensive fortifications, capable of splintering wood and reducing stone to gravel with a thunderous boom. Larger siege weapons of this type exist, and require entire teams of operators to position, load, and fire.

name: Cannonball
entries: Ammunition is required by a weapon that has {@itemProperty AF|XDMG|Ammunition}. A weapon's description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon.
Firearm ammunition is destroyed upon use.

name: Chromatic Rose
entriesTemplate: While this rose is held, it {{item.detail1}} as a harmless visual effect.

While holding the rose by its stem, you gain resistance to {{item.resist}} damage. If you would take more than 10 {{item.resist}} damage from a single source (after applying the resistance), the rose disintegrates, and you take no damage instead.

As an action, you can blow the petals from the rose to produce a 20-foot cone of {{item.resist}}. Each creature in the cone must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 3d10} {{item.resist}} damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Using this property destroys the rose.

name: Cleave
entries: If you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using this weapon, you can make a melee attack roll with the weapon against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon's damage, but don't add your ability modifier to that damage unless that modifier is negative. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

name: Double-Barrel Shotgun
entries: A classic design with two loaded barrels, the Double-Barrel Shotgun trades ammo capacity and range for reliability and firepower.

entries.name: Special
entries: If you attack with a double-bladed scimitar as part of the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can use a bonus action immediately after to make a melee attack with it. This attack deals {@damage 1d4} slashing damage on a hit, instead of {@damage 2d4}.

name: Dragon Scale Mail
entriesTemplate: Dragon Scale Mail is made of the scales of one kind of dragon. Sometimes dragons collect their cast-off scales and gift them. Other times, hunters carefully preserve the hide of a dead dragon. In either case, Dragon Scale Mail is highly valued.

While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 bonus to {@variantrule Armor Class|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against the breath weapons of Dragons, and you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to {{getFullImmRes item.resist}} damage.

Additionally, you can focus your senses as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to discern the distance and direction to the closest {{item.detail1}} dragon within 30 miles of yourself. This action can't be used again until the next dawn.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Firearm Bullet
entries: Firearm Bullets are destroyed upon use in a modern firearm.

name: Firearm Bullets (10)
entries: Firearm Bullets are destroyed upon use in a modern firearm.

name: Flare Gun
entries: Less a firearm and more a survival tool, a Flare Gun fires a single white-hot flare, useful for distress signals and last-ditch-effort self defense.

name: Flare
entries: Ammunition is required by a weapon that has {@itemProperty AF|XDMG|Ammunition}. A weapon's description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon.
Firearm ammunition is destroyed upon use.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Gaming Set
entries: If you have proficiency with a tool, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check too.

name: Gatling Gun
entries: Infamous for its rate of fire, the Gatling Gun rotates and fires six or more barrels in succession. This weapon is cumbersome, easily recognizable, and utterly terrifying.

name: Glaur
entries: Short, curved horns like a cornucopia. Played with valves, glaur sound like trumpets, while those without valves, known as gloon, have a more mournful sound.

name: Graze
entries: If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.

name: Grenade
entriesTemplate: As an action, you can either throw a grenade at a point up to 60 feet away or use a Grenade Launcher to propel the grenade to a point up to 1,000 feet away. The grenade explodes at that point, creating a particular effect in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}.

name: Hand Drum
entries: A double-headed skin drum fitted with handles along its side.

name: Handgun
entries: Portable, reliable, and with a generous magazine size, the Handgun is the go-to firearm for self-defense and law enforcement.

name: Hooked Shortspear
entries: On a hit with this weapon, the wielder can forgo dealing damage and attempt to trip the target, in which case the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or fall {@condition prone}. The DC is 8 + the wielder's Strength modifier + the wielder's proficiency bonus.

name: Hoopak
entries: A hoopak is a sturdy stick with a sling at one end and a pointed tip at the other.

entries.name: Special
entries: When you make a melee attack with this weapon, you ignore its ammunition property. You can use the hoopak as a martial ranged weapon. If you do, it uses the ammunition property, uses sling bullets, and deals {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage on a hit.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Hunting Rifle
entries: Designed for big game, Hunting Rifles are slow but accurate, requiring bolt action operation between every shot.

name: Instrument
entries: If you have proficiency with a tool, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check too.

name: Ioun Stone
entriesTemplate: Roughly marble sized, {@i Ioun Stones} are named after Ioun, a god of knowledge and prophecy revered on some worlds. Many types of {@i Ioun Stones} exist, each type a distinct combination of shape and color.

When you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to toss an {@i Ioun Stone} into the air, the stone orbits your head at a distance of {@dice 1d3} feet, conferring its benefit to you while doing so. You can have up to three {@i Ioun Stones} orbiting your head at the same time.

Each {@i Ioun Stone} orbiting your head is considered to be an object you are wearing. The orbiting stone avoids contact with other creatures and objects, adjusting its orbit to avoid collisions and thwarting all attempts by other creatures to attack or snatch it.

As a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action, you can seize and stow any number of {@i Ioun Stones} orbiting your head. If your {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to an Ioun Stone ends while it's orbiting your head, the stone falls as though you had dropped it.

name: Lance
entries: A Lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.

name: Light Repeating Crossbow
entries: This crossbow is fitted with a cartridge that can hold up to six crossbow bolts. It automatically reloads after firing until the cartridge runs out of ammunition. Reloading the cartridge takes an action.

name: Longhorn
entries: A Faerûnian flute of sophisticated make, found only in areas with skilled artisans, as in great cities or elven enclaves.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Magnum
entries: Chambered for large-caliber bullets, Magnum revolvers pack the most kick possible into a single shot.

name: Needle
entries: Blowgun needles are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Needles are typically stored in a {@item Pouch|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Needles (50)
entries: Blowgun needles are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Needles are typically stored in a {@item Pouch|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Nick
entries: When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action instead of as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Parlor Gun
entries: The smallest usable firearm, a Parlor Gun can be tucked into a stocking or hidden down a sleeve for two barrels of point-blank fire.

name: Pistol
entries: The earliest type of one-handed firearm, a Pistol operates as a miniature Musket, loading and firing a single heavy projectile with deadly results.

name: Potion of Resistance
entriesTemplate: When you drink this potion, you have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to {{getFullImmRes item.resist}} damage for 1 hour.

name: Pump Shotgun
entries: The Pump Shotgun's namesake is a distinctive sliding grip on the barrel which can be "pumped" to chamber a new round.

name: Push
entries: If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.

entries.name: Range
entries: A Range weapon has a range in parentheses after the Ammunition or Thrown property. The range lists two numbers. The first is the weapon's normal range in feet, and the second is the weapon's long range. When attacking a target beyond normal range, you have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the attack roll. You can't attack a target beyond the long range.

name: Revolver
entries: An iconic handgun, the Revolver stores six bullets in a rotating cylinder. This weapon is favored by Gunslingers for its reliability and stopping-power.

name: Ring of Resistance
entriesTemplate: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to {{getFullImmRes item.resist}} damage while wearing this ring. The ring is set with {{item.detail1}}.

name: Sap
entries: If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

name: Scroll of Protection
entriesTemplate: Using a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to read the scroll creates a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you. For 5 minutes, {{item.detail2}} can't enter or affect anything in the area. However, if you move in such a way that an {{item.detail1}} would be inside the area, the effect ends.

As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, a creature within 5 feet of the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} can attempt to overcome it, which forces the creature to make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. On a successful save, the creature ceases to be affected by the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Shell
entries: Ammunition is required by a weapon that has {@itemProperty AF|XDMG|Ammunition}. A weapon's description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon.
Firearm ammunition is destroyed upon use.

name: Shot
entries: Ammunition is required by a weapon that has {@itemProperty AF|XDMG|Ammunition}. A weapon's description specifies the type of ammunition used by the weapon.
Firearm ammunition is destroyed upon use.

name: Sling Bullet
entries: Sling bullets are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Sling Bullets are typically stored in a {@item Pouch|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Sling Bullets (20)
entries: Sling bullets are used with a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.
Sling Bullets are typically stored in a {@item Pouch|XPHB} (bought separately).

name: Slow
entries: If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to it, you can reduce its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduction doesn't exceed 10 feet.

name: Sniper Rifle
entries: An instrument of ranged precision, the Sniper Rifle can take shots from distant, nearly invisible ranges.

name: Songhorn
entries: A recorder, a simple type of flute, usually carved from wood.

name: Spiked Armor
entries: Spiked armor is a rare type of medium armor made by dwarves. It consists of a leather coat and leggings covered with spikes that are usually made of metal.

name: Submachine Gun
entries: Firing lighter, easier to control rounds, the Submachine Gun is an effective alternative to larger automatic weapons.

name: Tantan
entries: A tambourine, a popular instrument with halflings and humans south of the Dalelands.

name: Thelarr
entries: Also known as a whistlecane, a simple and easy-to-make wind instrument cut from a reed. They are so simple, in fact, that skilled bards frequently make and give them away to children—to the parents' delight or regret.

name: Tocken
entries: A hanging set of carved oval bells, usually played with a pair of light wooden hammers (or open handed). They are most common in underground cultures, where the resonant tones can carry.

name: Tool
entries: If you have proficiency with a tool, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to any ability check you make that uses the tool. If you have proficiency in a skill that's used with that check, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the check too.

name: Topple
entries: If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.

name: Trade Bar
entries: Because large numbers of coins can be difficult to transport and account for, many merchants prefer to use trade bars—ingots of precious metals and alloys (usually silver). These bars are valued by weight.

name: Trade Good
entries: Merchants commonly exchange trade goods without using currency.

name: Treasure (Art Object)
entries: Idols cast of solid gold, necklaces studded with precious stones, paintings of ancient kings, bejeweled dishes—art objects include all these and more.

name: Treasure (Coinage)
entries: Characters often find coins on their adventures and can spend those coins in shops, inns, and other businesses. Coins come in different denominations based on the relative worth of their material. The Coin Values table lists coins and how much they're worth relative to the Gold Piece, which is the game's main coin. For example, 100 Copper Pieces are worth 1 Gold Piece.
A coin weighs about a third of an ounce, so fifty coins weigh a pound.

name: Treasure (Gemstone)
entries: Gemstones are small, lightweight, and easily secured compared to their same value in coins.

entries.name: Speed
entries: A ship sailing against a strong wind moves at half speed. In a dead calm (no wind), waterborne ships can't move under sail and must be rowed. Keelboats and Rowboats are used on lakes and rivers. If going downstream, add the speed of the current (typically 3 miles per hour) to the speed of the vehicle. These vehicles can't be rowed against any significant current, but they can be pulled upstream by draft animals on the shores. A Rowboat can be carried and weighs 100 pounds.

entries.name: Crew
entries: A ship larger than a Keelboat or Rowboat needs a crew of skilled hirelings (see "{@book Services|XPHB|6|Services}") to function. The minimum number of skilled hirelings needed to crew a ship depends on the type of ship.

entries.name: Passengers
entries: The number of Small and Medium passengers the ship can accommodate using hammocks. A ship outfitted with private accommodations can carry one-fifth as many passengers. A passenger usually pays 5 SP per day for a hammock, but prices can vary from ship to ship. A small private cabin usually costs 2 GP per day.

entries.name: Damage Threshold
entries: See the {@variantrule Damage Threshold|XPHB|rules glossary}.

entries.name: Ship Repair
entries: Repairs to a damaged ship can be made while the vessel is berthed. Repairing 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} of damage requires 1 day and costs 20 GP for materials and labor. If the repairs are made in a location where supplies and skilled labor are abundant, such as a city shipyard, the repair time and cost are halved.

name: Vex
entries: If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

items.name: Ability:
entries: Charisma

items.name: Utilize:
entries: Play a known tune ({@dc 10}), or improvise a song ({@dc 15})

name: Wargong
entries: A metal gong, traditionally made from a shield, particularly the shield of an enemy. Both goblins and dwarves make and play wargongs, their sound echoing through tunnels in the Underdark.

name: Yklwa
entries: A yklwa (pronounced YICK-ul-wah) is a simple melee weapon that is the traditional weapon of Chultan warriors. A yklwa consists of a 3-foot wooden shaft with a steel or stone blade up to 18 inches long. Although it has the thrown weapon property, the yklwa is not well balanced for throwing.

name: Zulkoon
entries: A complex pump organ that originated with the zulkirs of Thay, who use it in the casting of their spells. It is considered to have a dramatic, but sinister, sound.
